summary.para: The hierarchy data model that the screen reader uses to navigate and interact with a Unity application.
The root nodes of the hierarchy.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityHierarchy.AddNode(System.String,UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode)

summary.para: Creates a new AccessibilityNode with the given label and adds it to the hierarchy under the given parent.

member.param: A label that succinctly describes the node.
The parent of the new node, or null if the node should be placed at the root level.

returns.para: The node created and added to the accessibility hierarchy.

summary.para: Resets the hierarchy to an empty state, removing all nodes and the screen reader focus.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityHierarchy.ContainsNode(UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode)

summary.para: Verifies whether the given node exists in the accessibility hierarchy.

member.param: The node to search for in the hierarchy.

returns.para: true if the node exists in this hierarchy and false otherwise.

summary.para: Initializes and returns an empty AccessibilityHierarchy.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityHierarchy.GetLowestCommonAncestor(UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode,UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode)

summary.para: Retrieves the lowest common ancestor of two nodes in the accessibility hierarchy.

member.param: The first node to find the lowest common ancestor of.
The second node to find the lowest common ancestor of.

returns.para: The lowest common ancestor of the two given nodes, or null if they don't have a common ancestor.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityHierarchy.InsertNode(System.Int32,System.String,UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode)

summary.para: Creates a new AccessibilityNode with the given label and inserts it at the given index in the hierarchy, under the given parent.

member.param: A zero-based index which provides the position of the new node in the parent's child list or in the list of root nodes if the node should be a root. If the index is invalid, then the node is added to the end of the parent's child list.
A label that succinctly describes the node.
The parent of the new node, or null if you want the node to be at the root level.

returns.para: The node created and inserted into the accessibility hierarchy.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityHierarchy.MoveNode(UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode,UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode,System.Int32)

summary.para: Moves the node elsewhere in the accessibility hierarchy. For example, under a different parent or at a different position in the parent's child list.

member.param: The node to move.
The new parent of the node, or null if the node should be placed at the root level.

An optional zero-based index which provides the position of the moved node in the new parent's child list or in the list of root nodes if the node should be a root. If the index is not provided or is invalid, then the node is moved at the end of the new parent's child list.

returns.para: true if the node was successfully moved and false otherwise.

summary.para: Refreshes the AccessibilityNode.frame of all nodes in the accessibility hierarchy.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityHierarchy.RemoveNode(UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode,System.Boolean)

summary.para: Removes the node from the accessibility hierarchy and removes or re-parents its descendants.

member.param: The node to remove.
true if the node's descendants should also be removed, or false if they should be moved under the node's parent. Defaults to true.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityHierarchy.TryGetNode(System.Int32,UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode&)

summary.para: Tries to retrieve the AccessibilityNode with the given ID in the hierarchy.

member.param: The ID of the node to retrieve.
The valid node with the associated ID, or null if no such node exists in this hierarchy.

returns.para: true if a node is found and false otherwise.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityHierarchy.TryGetNodeAt(System.Single,System.Single,UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode&)

summary.para: Tries to retrieve the AccessibilityNode at the given screen coordinates.

member.param: The horizontal position on the screen.
The vertical position on the screen.
The node found at the given screen coordinates, or null if there is no node at that position.

returns.para: true if the node is found and false otherwise.

summary.para: A node in the AccessibilityHierarchy representing a visual element, such as a UI element or an element that is part of your game, that needs to be accessible to the screen reader.
Whether the accessibility node allows direct touch interaction while the screen reader is active.
The node's children in the accessibility hierarchy.
Event invoked when the user performs a "decrement" action when focused on the accessibility node.
Event invoked when the user performs a "dismiss" action when focused on the accessibility node.

returns.para: true if the action succeeds and false otherwise.

summary.para: Event invoked on the main thread when the accessibility node gains or loses screen reader focus.
The bounding rectangle of the accessibility node in screen coordinates.
Delegate that calculates the frame of the accessibility node, automatically keeping it up-to-date.

returns.para: The calculated frame of the accessibility node, in screen coordinates.

summary.para: Additional guidance or context for interacting with the accessibility node.
The unique identifier of the accessibility node.
Event invoked when the user performs an "increment" action when focused on the accessibility node.
Event invoked when the user performs an "activate" action when focused on the accessibility node.

returns.para: true if the action succeeds and false otherwise.

summary.para: Whether the accessibility node is exposed to screen readers. The default value is true.
Whether the accessibility node is currently focused by the screen reader.
A short description of the accessibility node.
The node's parent in the accessibility hierarchy.
The type of user interface element that the accessibility node represents.
Event invoked when the user performs a "scroll" action when focused on the accessibility node.

returns.para: true if the action succeeds and false otherwise.

summary.para: Deprecated. Use AccessibilityNode.invoked instead.
The status of the visual element that the accessibility node represents.
The value of the visual element that the accessibility node represents.
A hash used for comparisons.
Provides a debugging string.

returns.para: A string containing the accessibility node ID and generational version.

summary.para: Options for defining the role of an AccessibilityNode to screen readers.
The accessibility node behaves like a button.
The accessibility node is a container of other nodes (examples of containers include tab bars and scroll views).
The accessibility node behaves like a dropdown list.
The accessibility node behaves like a heading that divides content into sections, such as the title of a navigation bar.
The accessibility node behaves like an image.
The accessibility node behaves like a keyboard key.
The accessibility node has none of the predefined roles.
The accessibility node behaves like a scrollable container.
The accessibility node behaves like a search field.
The accessibility node behaves like a slider that allows continuous adjustment through a range of values.
The accessibility node behaves like static text that can't change.
The accessibility node behaves like an ordered list of tabs.
The accessibility node behaves like an item in an ordered list of tabs.
The accessibility node behaves like a text field.
The accessibility node behaves like a toggle.
Describes the direction of a scrolling action.
The user is scrolling backward.
The user is scrolling from top to bottom.
The user is scrolling forward.
The user is scrolling from right to left.
The user is scrolling from left to right.
The direction of the scrolling action is unknown.
The user is scrolling from bottom to top.
Access point to system accessibility settings on the user's device and to events that trigger when the user changes these settings.
Event invoked on the main thread when the user changes the bold text setting in the system settings.
Event invoked on the main thread when the user changes the closed captioning setting in the system settings.
The font scale set by the user in the system settings.
Event invoked on the main thread when the user changes the font scale in the system settings.
Whether bold text is enabled in the system settings.
Whether closed captioning is enabled in the system settings.
Options for defining the state of an AccessibilityNode to screen readers.
The visual element represented by the accessibility node is currently disabled and does not respond to user interaction.
The visual element represented by the accessibility node is currently expanded, such as an expanded dropdown list or menu.
The accessibility node is not in a predefined state.
The visual element represented by the accessibility node is currently selected, such as a selected table row or a selected button in a segmented control.
Access point to APIs that enable applications made with Unity to support assistive technologies.
The accessibility hierarchy that is currently accessible to screen readers.
Whether the screen reader is turned on or off on the user's device.
Event invoked on the main thread when the user changes the screen reader focus by navigating to a different accessibility node.
Service used to send accessibility notifications to the screen reader.
Event invoked on the main thread when the user turns the screen reader on or off.
Whether to override the screen reader status given by the operating system.
Options to determine the status of the screen reader.
The screen reader is considered disabled, regardless of the status given by the operating system.
The screen reader is considered enabled, regardless of the status given by the operating system.
The screen reader status is determined by the operating system.
Sends accessibility notifications to the screen reader.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.IAccessibilityNotificationDispatcher.SendAnnouncement(System.String)

summary.para: Requests the screen reader to convey an announcement.

member.param: The announcement text that the screen reader should read.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.IAccessibilityNotificationDispatcher.SendLayoutChanged(UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode)

summary.para: Notifies the screen reader that the layout of the screen changed and optionally requests it to move its focus to a given accessibility node.

member.param: An optional node to be focused by the screen reader after processing the notification. If a node is not provided and the currently focused node still exists, the screen reader does not change its focus.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.IAccessibilityNotificationDispatcher.SendPageScrolledAnnouncement(System.String,UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode)

summary.para: Notifies the screen reader that a scrolling action completed and optionally requests it to move its focus to a given accessibility node.

member.param: Text that describes the new scroll position. For example, "Tab 3 of 5" or "Page 19 of 27". This text is read by the screen reader after processing the notification.
An optional node to be focused by the screen reader after processing the notification.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.IAccessibilityNotificationDispatcher.SendScreenChanged(UnityEngine.Accessibility.AccessibilityNode)

summary.para: Notifies the screen reader that the screen changed considerably and optionally requests it to move its focus to a given accessibility node.

member.param: An optional node to be focused by the screen reader after processing the notification. If a node is not provided, the screen reader focuses on the first active node in the accessibility hierarchy.

summary.para: A class containing methods to assist with accessibility for users with different vision capabilities.

member.name: M:UnityEngine.Accessibility.VisionUtility.GetColorBlindSafePalette(UnityEngine.Color[],System.Single,System.Single)

summary.para: Gets a palette of colors that should be distinguishable for normal vision, deuteranopia, protanopia, and tritanopia.

member.param: An array of colors to populate with a palette.
Minimum allowable perceived luminance from 0 to 1. A value of 0.2 or greater is recommended for dark backgrounds.
Maximum allowable perceived luminance from 0 to 1. A value of 0.8 or less is recommended for light backgrounds.

returns.para: The number of unambiguous colors in the palette.

summary.para: The Accessibility module includes APIs and tools that facilitate the development of accessible user experiences in Unity.
